Title: Elucidation of the 1-phenethylisoquinoline pathway from an endemic conifer Cephalotaxus hainanensis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected from leaf crude protein activity; candidates mined from transcriptome built from leaves, roots, twigs and bark
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a, inducible by IPTG in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro feeding with dopamine plus 4-hydroxydihydrocinnamaldehyde at 45 °C; crude leaf protein assay in C. hainanensis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; substrate feeding assay; LC-MS product confirmation; crude protein assay from leaves; site-directed mutagenesis; CD spectroscopy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold and defines the branch point diverging PIA biosynthesis from BIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Committed precursor for phenylethylisoquinoline alkaloids including cephalotaxine-related medicinal alkaloids such as homoharringtonine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChPSS was the only Pr10/Bet v1 candidate that converted dopamine and 4-HDCA into product 15 with diagnostic m/z 286.14319. 2. Product 15 was also detected from C. hainanensis leaves and from crude leaf protein extracts supplied with precursors, supporting in planta relevance. 3. ChPSS also accepted 4-HPAA and dopamine to produce (S)-norcoclaurine, showing substrate promiscuity but establishing its main role in PIA scaffold formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key gateway enzyme for reconstructing the early PIA pathway in heterologous hosts for sustainable production of cephalotaxine-derived medicinal alkaloids; useful for pathway stacking with upstream dopamine and 4-HDCA modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate promiscuity may produce off-pathway benzylisoquinoline alkaloid intermediates depending on aldehyde availability
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: First committed step toward 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a Pictet–Spengler condensation joining dopamine and 4-HDCA to form the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the committed scaffold for the target alkaloid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key branch/committed-step enzyme; described as key enzyme driving Pictet–Spengler condensation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows convergent evolution relative to canonical NCS proteins despite structural similarity; also produces (S)-norcoclaurine from 4-HPAA and dopamine; key residues supported by mutagenesis include Tyr86, Glu70, and Asp122-related positions

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a, inducible by IPTG in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro feeding with tyrosine or L-DOPA; coexpression with ChPPO1 for tyrosine-to-dopamine conversion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with substrate feeding; coexpression assay with ChPPO1; HPLC/LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies tyramine and dopamine precursor flux by decarboxylating tyrosine and L-DOPA in the dopamine branch of PIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immediate amine substrate for Pictet–Spengler condensation leading to the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChTyDC1 was identified as an aromatic amino acid decarboxylase candidate in C. hainanensis. 2. Recombinant enzyme decarboxylated both tyrosine and L-DOPA, consistent with dual substrate AAADC activity. 3. Coexpression with ChPPO1 enabled complete biosynthesis of dopamine from tyrosine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream module to provide dopamine precursor in reconstructed microbial or plant PIA pathways, especially when paired with ChPPO1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad substrate acceptance may affect flux partitioning among tyramine-, dopamine-, or other aromatic amine-derived pathways
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aromatic amino acid decarboxylase (AAADC); tyrosine/DOPA decarboxylase
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylethylisoquinoline alkaloid precursor supply; dopamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tyrosine to tyramine and L-DOPA to dopamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the target scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper text mentions coexpression construct naming inconsistently in Methods, but Results specifically validate ChTyDC1

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a, inducible by IPTG in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro feeding with tyrosine or tyramine; coexpression with ChTyDC1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with substrate feeding; coexpression with ChTyDC1; HPLC and LC-MS metabolite confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the aromatic hydroxylase generating L-DOPA from tyrosine and dopamine from tyramine, enabling dopamine biosynthesis for PIA production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immediate amine substrate for ChPSS-mediated formation of the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Of two PPO candidates, ChPPO1 converted tyrosine to L-DOPA. 2. ChPPO1 also converted tyramine to dopamine. 3. Coexpression with ChTyDC1 yielded complete dopamine biosynthesis from tyrosine, establishing PPO-mediated hydroxylation in this pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical upstream engineering part for dopamine supply in heterologous PIA biosynthesis, especially where CYP71AD-type hydroxylases are absent
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PPO-type enzymes may oxidize other phenolics, potentially causing undesired side reactions or browning-related metabolic effects in plant systems
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylethylisoquinoline alkaloid precursor supply; dopamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tyrosine to L-DOPA and tyramine to dopamine in the proposed dopamine biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the target scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed as the elusive aromatic hydroxylase in C. hainanensis, replacing a CYP71AD-type enzyme not found in the transcriptome

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast feeding with cinnamic acid or phenylpropyl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; substrate feeding assay; HPLC and LC-MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid, supporting the hydroxylation-first route toward 4-HDCA and PIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aldehyde substrate for ChPSS to generate the committed 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChC4H1 catalyzed cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in yeast assays. 2. ChC4H1 did not convert phenylpropyl aldehyde to 4-HDCA, arguing against the hydroxylation-last model. 3. Results support a hydroxylation-first route shared with monolignol biosynthesis upstream of PIA-specific branching.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as part of the upstream aldehyde supply module when reconstructing the 4-HDCA branch in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a core phenylpropanoid enzyme, flux redirection may interfere with lignin/phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the target aldehyde branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three active C4Hs in the study; activity on cinnamic acid supports shared early steps with lignin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast feeding with cinnamic acid or phenylpropyl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; substrate feeding assay; HPLC and LC-MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid, supporting the hydroxylation-first route toward 4-HDCA and PIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aldehyde substrate for ChPSS to generate the committed 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChC4H2 catalyzed cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in yeast assays. 2. ChC4H2 did not hydroxylate phenylpropyl aldehyde to 4-HDCA. 3. These results support the hydroxylation-first pathway model for 4-HDCA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative C4H module for engineering upstream 4-HDCA biosynthesis in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May perturb central phenylpropanoid and lignin metabolism when overexpressed in plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the target aldehyde branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three active C4Hs in the study

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast feeding with cinnamic acid or phenylpropyl aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; substrate feeding assay; HPLC and LC-MS product detection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid, supporting the hydroxylation-first route toward 4-HDCA and PIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Aldehyde substrate for ChPSS to generate the committed 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChC4H3 catalyzed cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in yeast assays. 2. ChC4H3 failed to convert phenylpropyl aldehyde to 4-HDCA. 3. These results reinforce the hydroxylation-first pathway model.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative C4H enzyme option for pathway reconstruction of 4-HDCA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter endogenous phenylpropanoid flux in plant engineering contexts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid at the 4-position to form p-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for the target aldehyde branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three active C4Hs in the study

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a, inducible by IPTG in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro feeding with p-coumaroyl aldehyde or cinnamaldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with substrate feeding; HPLC and LC-MS product confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces p-coumaroyl aldehyde to 4-HDCA, creating the PIA-specific branch that diverges from lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct aldehyde substrate for ChPSS-mediated synthesis of the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChDBR2 converted p-coumaroyl aldehyde (5) to 4-HDCA (9) in vitro. 2. ChDBR2 did not convert cinnamaldehyde (7) to phenylpropyl aldehyde (8), rejecting the hydroxylation-last model. 3. ChDBR2 marks the key divergence point from lignin-related phenylpropanoid metabolism into the PIA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical branch-forming enzyme for engineering selective 4-HDCA production and routing flux toward PIAs in microbes or plants
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with lignin/phenylpropanoid aldehyde pools may affect structural phenylpropanoid metabolism in plants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the side-chain double bond of p-coumaroyl aldehyde to form 4-HDCA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate aldehyde substrate for the target scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly identifies DBR as crucial step where PIA biosynthesis diverges from lignin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from transcriptome; cloned from pooled leaf, phloem and root cDNA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET28a, inducible by IPTG in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro feeding with p-coumaroyl aldehyde or cinnamaldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assay with substrate feeding; HPLC and LC-MS product confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces p-coumaroyl aldehyde to 4-HDCA, contributing to the PIA-specific branch diverging from lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Direct aldehyde substrate for ChPSS-mediated synthesis of the 1-phenethylisoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ChDBR3 converted p-coumaroyl aldehyde (5) to 4-HDCA (9) in vitro. 2. ChDBR3 did not accept cinnamaldehyde (7) to produce phenylpropyl aldehyde (8). 3. ChDBR3 supports the hydroxylation-first route and the branch from lignin-related metabolism to PIA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative/parallel DBR catalyst for engineering 4-HDCA supply to PIA pathways
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux diversion from phenylpropanoid aldehydes may influence lignification-related metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Full-length transcriptome (Iso-Seq) and Illumina RNA-seq correction; NCBI SRA SRX12392777
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the side-chain double bond of p-coumaroyl aldehyde to form 4-HDCA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate aldehyde substrate for the target scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts similarly to ChDBR2 in the in vitro assays
